provenance: docs/art/TILE-ART-SPEC.md governs this file's schema (section 7 fields, section 11 tile vocabulary, section 12 cosmetic variants); section 8 is the pack-swap procedure this file enacts. DECISIONS.md art register, SIXTH revision (Eli 2026-07-15): the fifth revision's true-black void + Roman-facade infrastructure was correct, but its dominant-tile SELECTION off the Kenney 1-Bit sheet read as an ugly low-contrast ICON-NOISE carpet (the 1-Bit pack is an atlas of 1024 tiny distinct glyphs, not a seamless terrain tileset). This revision keeps ALL the fifth-revision infrastructure (true-black #000000 void, DEFAULT_ART_DIR=kenney, the three civic-facade materials, the cosmetic-variant hash, the light/peek pipeline byte-identical) and re-SKINS only which sheet the dominant surfaces draw from -- swapping the atlas to Kenney's Roguelike Modern City Pack (CC0, content/art/kenney/CityPack/License.txt), whose Tilemap is a genuine seamless masonry terrain set: solid low-frequency red-brick / grey-ashlar / warm-sandstone coursing walls and grey/tan flagstone + tilled-earth + grass floors that tile into calm, weighty, architectural masses reading as SOLID stone (the Dwarf-Fortress-mass muse + Eli's gritty-Rome cue), NOT a restless field of icons.
notes: SIXTH revision, the tile-iteration pass. Changed vs the fifth revision: (1) atlas -> art/kenney/CityPack/tilemap_packed.png (592x448 = 37x28 @16px, zero spacing -- the SheetAtlasSpec grid mechanism is unchanged, only the sheet and cell coords differ; no Java change). (2) sheet.columns/rows -> 37/28. (3) Every wall_*/floor_*/roof_*/water/facade_*/slope/stair region re-pointed at City-Pack cells hand-picked as the INTERIOR (border-free) cells of each building block so they tile seamlessly (verified by tiling proofs into masses, regenerable in tools/scratch): grey ashlar (cols 5-6 rows 6-9), warm sandstone/travertine (cols 9-10 rows 6-9), red brick (cols 1-2 rows 6-10), tan flagstone (cols 3-4 rows 19-22), grey flagstone (cols 0-2 rows 19-20), pure tilled earth (cols 4-5 row 24 + col 11 row 26), pure grass (cols 0-1 row 24), clean open pool-water (cols 29-30 rows 4-5), striped market-awning canvas (cols 28-29 rows 10-11), and three grand multi-register stone FACADE elevation blocks matched by stone colour (grey cols 8-10 / red-brick cols 4-6 / sandstone cols 24-26, rows 0-2). (4) Palette-unification tints pushed to warm-gritty-stone: dirt gains an earthy #B0895A multiply; ash retuned #9E968A; the two timber ids gain warm-brown multiplies over sandstone courses (the City Pack ships NO seamless wood surface -- every wood cell is a discrete crate on transparency that tiles into gaps, so oak/trudgeon route onto solid sandstone shapes tinted brown = solid timber-framed masses, a deliberate compromise flagged for Eli, low-impact in this stone/brick Docks district); glowstone moved onto red brick (wall_brick/floor_cobble) where its eerie-red glow reads best. (5) The three fifth-revision facade region NAMES (facade_cornice/facade_colonnade/facade_baluster) are replaced by stone-matched facade_granite/facade_brick/facade_reman -- a single shared-hue portico cell cannot serve grey AND red AND sandstone facades, so each stone gets its own elevation block; the three civic facade materials' byAppearance update accordingly. Every OTHER region-name in the vocabulary is preserved so material->region wiring is intact. (6) fluids.water gains a #4E9AB0 tint (honored by the fluid render path, JsonTileArtResolver.fluidTintRgb) deepening the bright pool-cyan to a gritty harbor teal. voidColor/#000000, lightTintQ8, zPeekDimQ8, tilePx=16 are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the fifth revision. Caveat (unchanged from the fifth revision, still true): chromatis/lightstone/ice have no baked signature-hue slope cell, so their ramps/stairs render in slope_a/stair_rung's native grey -- low impact, none are placed on the Docks surface map today.
regionsProvenance: Cells hand-picked from CityPack/tilemap_packed.png (37x28 @16px) by zoom-reading the actual sheet and pixel-sampling candidate cells, then proving each region tiles into a solid low-frequency mass under the real cosmetic-variant hash (TILE-ART-SPEC section 12). Wall blocks sit at rows 5-12: red brick cols 0-3, grey ashlar cols 4-7, warm sandstone cols 8-11; the picks avoid the border/edge columns (0/3/4/7/8/11) and take only interior cells so coursing tiles seamlessly. Floor blocks sit at rows 19-27: grey flagstone cols 0-2, tan flagstone cols 3-5, tilled earth cols 8-14 rows 25-27, grass cols 0-1 row 24; floor_earth/floor_moss use the pure solid-fill cells at row 24 (0/1 grass, 4/5 dirt) plus one speckled center to kill the grass-to-dirt autotile edge grid. water/wall_crystal/floor_crystal use the pool's clean open-water cells (cols 29-30 rows 4-5), avoiding the grey stone pool rim. Facades are the three grand multi-register elevation blocks at rows 0-3 (grey cols 8-15, red-brick cols 0-7, sandstone cols 24-31), interior cells rows 0-2 -- these tile into ornate windowed ashlar reading unambiguously as a civic building frontage, distinct from plain wall_stone (the honest 'grand elevation, not free-standing columns' Rome cue: no literal fluted-column/arch asset exists in this pack -- its vertical-rhythm cells cols 12-19 are modern glass storefronts, not arcades). slope_a/stair_rung reuse plain ashlar/flagstone cells (no diagonal-slope or stair tile exists on this sheet; ramps/stairs render as solid sloped stone -- low impact, they are rare). missing = (24,7), an off-terrain prop cell that reads as clearly-wrong if it ever surfaces.
voidColorNotes: True black, unchanged from the fifth revision (Eli 2026-07-15 'completely black background'). Fills cells beyond max z-peek depth (unbuilt/unlit space), not a lit ground tile. ObserverApp.java's ScreenUtils.clear(...) reads this value dynamically every boot (TILE-ART-SPEC section 5.2) -- no second hardcoded copy to keep in sync.

granite.notes: Common default building stone and the district's DOMINANT material -- so its tint sets the whole city's cast. The City Pack's coursed-ashlar cell bakes cool grey; a warm travertine #D8C6A2 multiply pushes it to a Roman warm-cream dressed stone, which (because granite is everywhere in the Docks geometry) carries the entire district into the warm-gritty-stone register the brief demands rather than reading as a monochrome grey city. Still distinct from reman_concrete (different coursing SHAPE + reman's warmer native sandstone) and steel (cool tint). Baseline every other stone-family material is judged against.

granite_facade.provenance: Civic-only material, minted via the add-material-raw skill as a physical clone of granite (content/raws/materials/granite_facade.json) -- identical density/hardness/conductivity/etc., different id so it can carry its OWN art mapping without repainting every plain granite wall in the district.
granite_facade.notes: Rome cue (DECISIONS.md sixth revision): used ONLY for the Weighhouse (K01) and Mission of the Flame (K17) street-facing frontage walls (gen_docks_surface.py tags just those edges). Draws the grand grey multi-register stone elevation block (facade_granite) -- an ornate windowed ashlar frontage, a stronger and palette-coherent Rome cue than the fifth revision's weak 1-Bit portico glyph, which the brief itself flagged as inadequate. No literal free-standing colonnade/arch cell exists on this sheet.

dirt.notes: Earthy packed ground. Wall/block draws the sandstone coursing shape (wall_rubble); floor draws the City Pack's native tilled-earth cells (floor_earth). The #B0895A earthy multiply pushes both toward warm packed-earth and keeps dirt distinct from reman_concrete's clean tan sandstone (which shares the same coursing shape family).

oak.notes: The City Pack ships no seamless wood-surface cell (every wood cell is a discrete crate/board on transparency that tiles into gaps), so oak routes onto the solid sandstone coursing shape (wall_plank) with a warm-brown #B5793B multiply, reading as a solid timber-framed masonry mass. Deliberate compromise (DECISIONS.md sixth revision, flagged for Eli): trades literal wood-grain for solidity, the right call under the DF-mass muse + single-atlas constraint.

thatch.notes: Roofing straw: no gold/straw tileable surface exists in the City Pack, so a straw-gold #E8D67A multiply over the tan flagstone shape (roof_thatch) supplies the hue -- a solid golden-tan roof mass.

trudgeon_wood.notes: Regional timber, lore-distinct from oak. Same solid-sandstone-shape compromise as oak (no wood surface exists); a darker warm-brown #A5672E multiply keeps the two woods visually distinct as timber-framed masses.

trudgeon_wood@getilia_soak.notes: Treatment-derived id minted at load (getilia_soak); the '@' is verbatim in the material key but not a filesystem path (regions are grid coords, not files). Darker #8A5626 multiply than raw trudgeon_wood so the getilia treatment reads as a visible change.

steel.notes: Coursed/riveted plate reads as banded masonry. Shares the sandstone coursing shape (wall_masonry) with reman_concrete; a cool blue-grey #AEB8C2 multiply pulls it to metal, distinct from reman's warm native sandstone.

brick.notes: No tint: the City Pack's native red-brick coursing (wall_brick) is exactly what brick should look like -- solid, low-frequency terracotta courses.

brick_facade.provenance: Civic-only material, minted via the add-material-raw skill as a physical clone of brick (content/raws/materials/brick_facade.json).
brick_facade.notes: Rome cue: used ONLY for the King's Bond (K12) street-facing frontage wall. Draws the grand red-brick multi-register elevation block (facade_brick) -- a monumental windowed brick frontage matching King's Bond's bonded-warehouse character.

chromatis.provenance: heatGlowTint is the discharge/saturation overlay read by JsonTileArtResolver.heatGlowTintRgb (BLESSING-QUEUE ruling 5) -- untouched by this pass.
chromatis.notes: No base tint: wall_crystal/floor_crystal are the City Pack's genuinely-baked blue pool-water cells, close enough to canon silver-blue to trust as-is. Ramp/stair fall back to slope_a/stair_rung's native grey (no baked-blue slope cell exists) -- a stated, low-impact caveat since chromatis is not placed on the Docks surface map today.

chromatis_melt.notes: Molten/cooling chromatis reads gold. No gold tileable surface exists, so this reuses the grey ashlar shape (a 'melted stone' silhouette) with a warm gold #D9A83C multiply rather than the blue crystal shape, which would multiply toward a muddy green.

phorys.notes: Crystalline/veined mineral: kept on the grey ashlar shape (wall_hatch) with a teal-cyan #6FB0C0 multiply so phorys keeps its own identity distinct from the plain grey stone materials and from chromatis/ice's blue pool cells.

lightstone.notes: Chargeable emissive natural mineral: the City Pack's native solid-green grass cell (wall_moss/floor_moss) reads as a natural glowing stone, so no tint. If colorStops ship, add per-bucket regions later; bucket clamp keeps the single region correct until then. Ramp/stair fall back to slope_a/stair_rung's native grey (no baked-green slope cell exists) -- low impact (not placed on the Docks surface map today).

lightstone_shards.notes: Shares lightstone's green cells; a yellow-green #BFD05A multiply keeps shards visually distinct from whole lightstone.

glowstone.provenance: WorldBible section 9: eerie red light. minLight is the cosmetic observer clamp (TILE-ART-SPEC section 5.1).
glowstone.notes: Moved onto the red-brick shape (wall_brick / floor_cobble) where the canon eerie-red #E5433B multiply reads best as a glowing mineral -- red glow over red brick, distinct from the earthy dirt/ash it used to share a region with. Keeps minLight 8.

ash.notes: Shares the sandstone coursing shape (wall_rubble) with dirt; a muted grey-brown #9E968A multiply reads as burnt/soot rather than raw earth.

ice.notes: No tint. SpriteBatch tinting is strictly multiplicative (can only darken or re-hue, never lighten past the baked color), so there is no honest tint that makes the already-saturated baked-blue pool cell paler. The genuinely-baked blue is used as-is. Ramp/stair fall back to slope_a/stair_rung's native grey for the same no-baked-blue-slope reason as chromatis/lightstone.

reman_concrete.provenance: Reman engineering = concrete-equivalent, the wealth-high compound material (canon-trojian-compounds).
reman_concrete.notes: No tint: the City Pack's warm sandstone/travertine coursing (wall_masonry) IS the Rome-travertine mass this material wants -- injecting warm cream stone into the wealthy compounds. Differs from granite by SHAPE and warmth (sandstone coursing vs granite's grey ashlar) and from steel (which shares this region) by steel's added cool tint.

reman_facade.provenance: Civic-only material, minted via the add-material-raw skill as a physical clone of reman_concrete (content/raws/materials/reman_facade.json).
reman_facade.notes: Rome cue: used ONLY for the Quayward Compound's (C1) gate wall. Draws the grand warm-sandstone multi-register elevation block (facade_reman) -- a monumental travertine gate frontage, the palette-coherent Rome cue for the wealthy compound.

leather.notes: Striped market-awning canvas shape (roof_cloth) reads as stretched fabric. Dim warm-brown #8A6B44 multiply makes it deliberately poorer/dimmer than reman_concrete and than cloth's slightly lighter tint, per the canon wealth gradient.

cloth.notes: Same striped-awning shape (roof_cloth) as leather; a slightly lighter dim-tan #9C8560 multiply keeps cloth and leather as two distinguishable slum materials.

water.notes: The City Pack's pool-water cells are baked bright cyan; a #4E9AB0 secondary multiply (honored by the fluid render path, JsonTileArtResolver.fluidTintRgb) deepens them to a gritty harbor teal that reads better against the black void than raw cyan.
